input.command:
# Generate CA and server certificates
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/es-ca.key 2048 2>&1
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key /tmp/es-ca.key -out /tmp/es-ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=Elasticsearch-CA/O=KARMA" 2>&1
echo "CA cert generated"

openssl genrsa -out /tmp/es-node.key 2048 2>&1
openssl req -new -key /tmp/es-node.key -out /tmp/es-node.csr \
  -subj "/CN=es-cluster/O=KARMA" 2>&1

cat > /tmp/es-san.ext << 'EOF'
[v3_req]
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = es-cluster-0.es-cluster
DNS.2 = es-cluster-1.es-cluster
DNS.3 = es-cluster-2.es-cluster
DNS.4 = es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = es-http
DNS.8 = es-http.elasticsearch
DNS.9 = es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.10 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

openssl x509 -req -days 3650 -in /tmp/es-node.csr \
  -CA /tmp/es-ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/es-ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/es-node.crt -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/es-san.ext 2>&1
echo "Node cert generated"
openssl verify -CAfile /tmp/es-ca.crt /tmp/es-node.crt

apiVersion: v1

    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: 1Gi
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms256m -Xmx256m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health \
                    | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 12
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: es-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: es-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
EOF

content.thinking:
The logs show "failed to resolve host [es-cluster-1.es-cluster]". This is because only es-cluster-0 pod is running - the other pods haven't started yet, so DNS resolution of es-cluster-1.es-cluster fails because the pod isn't there yet.

This is expected behavior - when a StatefulSet starts, pods come up one at a time. es-cluster-0 is waiting for es-cluster-1 and es-cluster-2 to start.

But wait - there's a problem. StatefulSets start pods sequentially - es-cluster-0 needs to be READY before es-cluster-1 starts. 

And that's where the deadlock happens: es-cluster-0's readiness probe checks cluster health, which requires all 3 nodes to form a quorum. With all nodes configured as masters, Elasticsearch needs at least 2 to elect a leader. But es-cluster-0 can't find the other nodes yet, and even with `cluster.initial_master_nodes` set to all three, it won't bootstrap with just itself - it's waiting for a quorum that can't form until the other pods start.

The solution is to use `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all three pods launch simultaneously instead of sequentially. That way they can discover each other and form a quorum right away. Readiness probes don't block pod startup or cluster formation—they only affect service routing—so the pods can establish a cluster even while the probes are failing. The real issue is the default sequential pod management, which prevents pod 1 from starting until pod 0 is ready. I need to switch to parallel pod management so all three pods can start simultaneously and form the cluster together.

Updating the StatefulSet with parallel pod management...

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: 1Gi
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms256m -Xmx256m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health \
                    | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 18
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: es-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: es-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
EOF

ES_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
# Test HTTPS cluster health via es-http service from curl-test pod
kubectl exec curl-test -n elasticsearch -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ES_PASS}" \
  https://es-http.elasticsearch:9200/_cluster/health?pretty 2>&1

content.text: Cluster is green with 3 nodes. Now test a write and read.

input.command:
ES_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)

# Write a document
kubectl exec curl-test -n elasticsearch -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ES_PASS}" \
  -X POST "https://es-http.elasticsearch:9200/test-index/_doc/1?pretty" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message": "karma-health-check", "status": "ok"}' 2>&1

ES_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)

# Read the document back
kubectl exec curl-test -n elasticsearch -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ES_PASS}" \
  "https://es-http.elasticsearch:9200/test-index/_doc/1?pretty" 2>&1
